fact: August 30 is exactly 242 days into the year, which means there are only 123 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for monarch butterflies to start their epic 3,000-mile journey south to Mexico - some of them are probably flying over your head right now!
The sun rises about 6 minutes later on August 30 than it did just two weeks earlier, and you can actually notice the difference if you pay attention!
Apple trees in many parts of North America are heavy with fruit right now, and some varieties are so ripe they're literally falling off the branches!
This date falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, making it one of the most 'Virgo-ish' days of the entire year for anyone who cares about zodiac signs.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, August 30 is like their version of late February - winter is almost over and you can smell spring in the air!

Crickets are chirping their loudest around this time of year in North America, and you can actually tell the temperature by counting their chirps - count for 14 seconds, add 40, and you've got the temperature in Fahrenheit!
The Earth is tilted at exactly the same angle it was on this date last year, but we're on the completely opposite side of our orbit around the sun!
Many deciduous trees have already started changing colors by August 30, even though most people think fall doesn't start until September - nature doesn't follow our calendar!
In northern regions like Canada and Alaska, some lakes and ponds can start developing their first thin layer of ice on really cold August 30th mornings!
The daylight on August 30 lasts almost exactly the same amount of time as it does in mid-April - but the sun's angle makes everything look completely different!
